destinations.evidence: The codec accepts exactly runtime u16 values 0, 1, and 2 and rejects every other value with InvalidFieldValue for agent_runtime.
PersistentAgentRequest decodes field 25 as a uint16 and immediately applies AgentRuntime::try_from, so invalid runtime values fail request decoding rather than defaulting.

equivalence_argument: The port preserves the codec boundary exactly: field 25 is decoded as agent_runtime, valid values map to the three represented runtimes, and all other u16 values return InvalidFieldValue.
rationale: The candidate directly identifies the current codec boundary for agent_runtime: only the three represented u16 values are accepted, and invalid values must fail rather than silently choosing a default. There are no in-cluster predecessor or successor candidates to collapse.
statement: Persistent agent precompile codec decoding must accept agent_runtime uint16 values 0, 1, and 2 as ZeroClaw, OpenClaw, and Hermes respectively, and must reject any other value with InvalidFieldValue for agent_runtime.
suggested_falsification_tests: Call AgentRuntime::try_from with 0, 1, and 2 and assert the values map to ZeroClaw, OpenClaw, and Hermes.
Call AgentRuntime::try_from with 3 and through PersistentAgentRequest::from_abi field 25 with an invalid u16, and assert decoding returns InvalidFieldValue for agent_runtime.
summary: Persistent agent runtime values are validated in the port codec.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Decode persistent-agent ABI inputs with agent_runtime values 0, 1, 2, and 3; only 0-2 should decode and 3 should return InvalidFieldValue for agent_runtime.
title: Agent runtime values are validated
